summary: First iteration full-stack testing of Avero. Backend: 10/10 pytest passing (enquiries CRUD, admin JWT auth, stats, PATCH, chat SSE with session/delta/done events). Frontend: landing loads, contact form submits successfully, admin login + dashboard + stat cards + tab filters work, chatbot streams real AI response from Emergent LLM, mobile viewport shows mobile CTA bar (Call/WhatsApp/Pricing/Contact) and mobile menu opens. Note: initial chat test failed with transient 'Budget exceeded' from Emergent LLM but succeeded on retry — flagging as flaky.

minor.issue: Occasional transient error from Emergent LLM: 'litellm.BadRequestError: Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0'. Passed on retry. Model used is 'gpt-5.4-mini' — worth verifying it is a supported Emergent model name.

action_items: Investigate transient 'Budget exceeded' error from Emergent LLM on /api/chat — verify 'openai/gpt-5.4-mini' is the correct model identifier for EMERGENT_LLM_KEY provisioning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses model 'gpt-5.4-mini' — confirm it is a valid Emergent-supported model, otherwise fallback to 'gpt-4o-mini' or similar.
Enquiry POST uses status_code 200 (default) instead of 201 for creation — minor semantic nit.
PATCH /api/admin/enquiries/{id} accepts raw dict without Pydantic validation — could add a StatusUpdate model with allowed enum values.
CORS origins default to '*' with allow_credentials=True — this combination is problematic if credentials are ever needed; consider explicit origins in prod.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 test enquiries (TEST_ prefix) via API + UI during testing; one was patched to status='contacted'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_avero_api.py covers all endpoints. Chat SSE test can be flaky due to Emergent LLM key budget errors — retry once if it fails. Admin credentials are seeded on startup.
rca of the issue: Chat flakiness RCA: emergentintegrations LlmChat returned litellm 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0'. This usually indicates the LLM key hasn't fully propagated budget for the requested model, or the model name isn't in the allowed list. Retry succeeded — suggests transient provisioning delay rather than a code bug. Recommend verifying model 'gpt-5.4-mini' availability on Emergent Universal LLM key.
